# Button-cohesion census — remaining off-system CTAs (2026-06-13)
**Status:** Open program. Generated by the `button-cohesion-census` workflow (read-only, Mobbin/canon-grounded) the night of 2026-06-13, after PR #405 landed Plan CTAs + Discover chips.
**Why this exists.** The button-system decision (`docs/decisions/2026-06-12-button-system-solid-primary.md`, ENG-1079) shipped the `SupprButton` primitive + a handful of call-sites (recipe, plan, a few Today). The census proves the *migration itself was never completed* — ~100 confirmed off-system text CTAs remain, and the completeness critic found **3 more signatures the first pass didn't even sweep** (see Coverage holes). True scope is ~150–200 CTAs across ~60 files. ENG-1079 + ENG-1080 were marked Done prematurely; this doc is the honest backlog.
**The rule (canon §button):** PRIMARY = solid aubergine pill, white sans label, exactly ONE per screen (FAB + paywall excepted). SECONDARY/TERTIARY = GHOST (transparent, no border, plum label). Outline pills, beige `colors.card`/`bg-card` slabs, and ad-hoc styled Pressables/`<button>` are all off-system.

## Needs decision (4)
All four turn on one canon gap: **there is no destructive `SupprButton` variant.**

- **Delete recipe** — The completeness round cited a 'Delete recipe' outline pill at library.tsx:771, but no such button exists at that location in the current file (only the single 'Go public' inline action at line 920 is present). Is this a stale/phantom finding, or does a destructive 'Delete recipe' CTA live elsewhere that needs a destructive-variant decision? Canon defines no destructive button variant — destructive-confirm buttons are explicitly the 'intentionally tinted' sanctioned case.
- **Delete account** — Destructive primary action (Danger zone, ~line 3271), currently an aubergine outline pill. Canon sanctions 'destructive-confirm buttons that are intentionally tinted' but defines no destructive SupprButton variant. Should destructive CTAs (a) get a dedicated destructive variant added to SupprButton, (b) stay hand-styled as intentionally-tinted destructive, or (c) migrate to ghost? Needs a destructive-treatment ruling that applies to the whole Danger-zone cluster.
- **Remove household** — Destructive household-leave action (~line 3390), aubergine outline pill. Same destructive-variant question as Delete account — resolve together with the destructive-treatment ruling.
- **Log out** — Account sign-out action (~line 3143), aubergine outline pill. Sign-out is destructive-adjacent but not data-destroying. Should it be ghost (treat as a normal secondary) or grouped with the destructive cluster? Lean ghost, but confirm alongside the destructive ruling.

## Sanctioned-leave (11) — confirmed NOT drift

- **Add ingredient** — Dashed-border add-row affordance (styles.addBtn line 850-854: borderStyle 'dashed', borderColor accent.primary+'50'). Th
- **Import from {name} (Instagram / TikTok / Blog SourceTile)** — Three icon+label option tiles for choosing an import source — option-selection affordances (OptionCard-style), not text 
- **Which one should I choose?** — Disclosure toggle (icon + underlined text link) that expands a help explainer. A disclosure affordance / plain-text link
- **How does age affect my target?** — Help-disclosure toggle (icon + underlined text link). Sanctioned disclosure / plain-text-link pattern, not a CTA.
- **Show the maths / Hide the maths** — Disclosure toggle (text link + chevron) that shows/hides the formula breakdown. Sanctioned disclosure pattern, not a CTA
- **Allergy chips (Peanuts, Tree nuts, Milk, etc.)** — Multi-select option chips using the §7 chip grammar (quiet-fill rest, primary-soft fill + plum label selected). Sanction
- **Pace preset buttons (Slow / Moderate / Fast)** — Multi-choice preset selector (active = tinted, rest = neutral) — a segmented/option-state selector, sanctioned per the s
- **I understand this is below the recommended safety floor (ack** — Checkbox control with conditional border/fill — a form control, not a text CTA. Sanctioned control pattern.
- **Already have an account? Sign in / Don't have an account? Cr** — Mode-toggle rendered as plain hint text (styles.hint, no pill/border/fill). Canon explicitly sanctions 'links rendered a
- **Forgot password?** — Plain hint-text link (styles.hint, no CTA affordance). Sanctioned per canon's 'links rendered as plain text'. (line ~561
- **Sign in with magic link** — Plain hint-text link (styles.hint with fontWeight 600 override, no pill/border/fill). Sanctioned plain-text link, not a 

## Coverage holes (completeness critic — NOT yet in the worklist above)

The first pass swept 3 signatures (mobile: `borderColor`+`primarySolid` outline, `backgroundColor: colors.card` fill; web: `border-primary-solid`/`bg-muted`). These were missed and need a census-pass-2:

- AUTH LANE entirely unswept (both platforms). apps/mobile/app/login.tsx carries three hand-styled pill CTAs the census signatures never match: clay-pill primary (backgroundColor: colors.tint, NOT colors.card), Apple ink button (backgroundColor: colors.text), and 'Continue with email' outline pill (borderWidth: 1.5 + borderColor: colors.border, NOT primarySolid). None is in the census-modified diff. Web auth equivalent lives via src/app/components/onboarding/steps/signup.tsx + src/context/AuthSessionContext.tsx — also unswept.
- ONBOARDING LANE unswept (24 mirrored step files on each platform). apps/mobile/components/onboarding/steps/welcome.tsx has a hand-styled 'Get started' Pressable (backgroundColor: colors.tint) and a 'Sign in' ghost link. scaffold.tsx uses accent.primary+'10' tinted slabs. The census's named decision (docs/decisions/2026-06-12-flat-card-surfaces.md) is about CARD elevation, not button cohesion, and scopes neither auth nor onboarding.
- ALTERNATE OUTLINE SIGNATURE missed: outline pills built from borderWidth: 1.5 + borderColor: colors.border (44 mobile files) are a distinct signature from the swept borderColor+primarySolid pattern. The retired-aubergine sweep would not flag these. Examples not in the census diff: cook.tsx, whats-new.tsx, profile.tsx, recipe/[id].tsx, create-recipe.tsx, RecipeEditSheet.tsx, OptionCard.tsx, GoalPaceEditorSheet/BodyFields.tsx.
- ALTERNATE FILL SIGNATURE missed: fill CTAs via backgroundColor: colors.tint or colors.text (clay/ink) are NOT colors.card fills, so the fill-as-CTA detector skips them. Confirmed in login.tsx (3), onboarding welcome, create-recipe.tsx (4), cook.tsx (5), whats-new.tsx (5).
- WEB INLINE-STYLE buttons missed: className-based greps (border-primary-solid / bg-muted) skip <button style={{...}}> inline-styled CTAs. ~19 src/app/components files carry them, including RecipeDetail.tsx, RecipeUpload.tsx, FastingTimer.tsx, ProgressDashboard.tsx, MealPlanner.tsx, ShoppingList.tsx, HouseholdPanel.tsx, MacroDetailPanel.tsx.
- WEB STYLED-<a>-AS-BUTTON missed: anchors styled as CTAs (src/app/App.tsx, UpgradePrompt.tsx, suppr/apple-health-card.tsx, onboarding/steps/signup.tsx) are not <button> and not the swept pill classes — invisible to a button-tag census.
- WEB FILL-CTA via bg-primary-solid / bg-clay (the new-system fill) appears in suppr/recipe-edit-dialog.tsx and suppr/override-ingredient-dialog.tsx — needs a parity pass to confirm these are already-migrated (sanctioned) vs ad-hoc.
- COOK MODE (mobile cook.tsx, web CookMode.tsx) and WHATS-NEW (whats-new.tsx) were named as likely-missed and are confirmed unswept; cook.tsx carries 5 alt-signature CTAs.
- PROFILE/ACCOUNT (apps/mobile/app/profile.tsx, src/app/components/Profile.tsx, Settings.tsx, SettingsBundleContent.tsx) carry borderWidth:1.5 outline rows + inline-style buttons not in the census diff.
- TouchableOpacity-vs-Pressable axis is a non-gap on MOBILE (only 3 TouchableOpacity uses, all in ui/collapsible.tsx — mobile is uniformly Pressable/PressableScale), but worth a one-line confirmation; the real mobile divergence is raw Pressable with inline style vs SupprButton primitive.

## Decision needed: destructive `SupprButton` variant

The canon has no destructive variant, but Delete account / Remove household / Log out (Settings danger zone) are currently aubergine outline pills and can't go solid-aubergine (that's the *primary* affordance — wrong signal for a destructive action).

**Recommendation (Claude, 2026-06-13 — flagged for Grace):** add `variant="destructive"` = **ghost grammar** (transparent, no border, same radius/padding) with the **destructive-red token label** (`Colors.destructive` / `--destructive`). Rationale: (1) matches the flat + ghost grammar — no new fill style; (2) iOS HIG convention is red *text* for destructive list actions, not a loud red slab; (3) these are deliberate-friction actions (you must mean it), so a quiet red ghost is the right weight; a solid-red slab would over-elevate "Log out". Delete account / Remove household → `destructive`. Log out → also `destructive` (red ghost) since it's destructive-adjacent and sits in the same danger cluster. The actual data-loss confirm (the modal "Delete" after the warning) MAY warrant solid-red — decide per-confirm. Implement the variant + decision doc before the danger-zone wave.

## Caveats — surfaces that are NOT mechanical (judgment required, do NOT blanket-migrate)

- **Auth lane (`login.tsx`, web `signup.tsx`).** "Continue with Apple" must stay **Apple-HIG-compliant** (Apple's required sign-in button styling — black/white ink, Apple logo), NOT a generic aubergine pill. Policy/review risk. The clay/ink/`colors.border` pills here are their own system; treat per Apple + the auth-screen design, not the generic primary/ghost rule.
- **Onboarding (24 mirrored step files / platform).** Has intentional divergence (see `project_onboarding_welcome_divergence`). The "Get started" / step CTAs may want the onboarding's own treatment. Route through the onboarding design, not a blanket sweep.

## Wave plan (each = one reviewable PR, pixel-verified per the cohesion gate)

Ordered by traffic × ambiguity-low-first. Non-destructive, unambiguous, high-traffic first:

1. **Wave A — Today dialogs + banners** (web `suppr/*-dialog.tsx`, `today-*` ; mobile `Today*Modal/Banner`): ~18 CTAs. Highest traffic, clear primary/ghost verdicts.
2. **Wave B — Library + Recipe (detail/upload)** web + mobile: ~14 CTAs.
3. **Wave C — Progress + Settings (non-danger) + Household**: ~16 CTAs.
4. **Wave D — Plan/Planner sheets + Food-search + Recap + Digest + Create-recipe**: ~16 CTAs.
5. **Wave E — Fasting + Health-sync + Targets + Weight + Profile + What's-new + Cook mode** (mostly the alt-`colors.border` signature): pending census-pass-2 count.
6. **Wave F — Destructive cluster** (after the variant decision lands).
7. **Wave G — Auth + Onboarding** (judgment lanes, smallest blast radius last).

**Before executing:** run census-pass-2 for the 3 unswept signatures (critic gave concrete targets) so each wave's file list is complete and we don't re-touch files. Then each wave: migrate → repoint guard tests → pre-sweep both suites → cohesion-gate pixel check → PR → merge.


---

## Census-pass-2 results (alternate signatures — completed 2026-06-13)
**29 net-new migrate** (12 web / 17 mobile) + **10 judgment-lane** + 9 sanctioned false-positives. **Combined confirmed migrate scope: ~127 CTAs.** Biggest net-new cluster: Cook mode (13).

**Apple sign-in (login.tsx, signup.tsx):** stays Apple-HIG (ink fill + Apple logo) — do NOT genericise. **Onboarding permissions 'Not now' / import 'Import this recipe':** off-system per canon but live in the intentional-divergence lane → route through onboarding design.

### Pass-1/pass-2 file overlap (do each ONCE, both passes together)
`apps/mobile/app/cook.tsx`, `src/app/components/CookMode.tsx`, `apps/mobile/components/onboarding/steps/permissions.tsx`, `apps/mobile/app/whats-new.tsx`.

### Revised wave plan (file-clustered; each file done once for both passes)

1. **Wave A — Today surfaces** (web `suppr/today-*` + `*-dialog.tsx`; mobile `Today*Modal/Banner`, `today-meals-section`, `north-star-block`): ~18. Highest traffic, easiest to pixel-verify (mostly inline).
2. **Wave B — Cook mode** (`cook.tsx` + `CookMode.tsx`, both platforms): ~15. Self-contained; ad-hoc solids → SupprButton primary, tinted pills → ghost.
3. **Wave C — Recipe + create + sheets** (RecipeDetail, RecipeUpload, recipe/[id], RecipeEditSheet, Override/AddIngredientSheet, create-recipe): ~14.
4. **Wave D — Library + Progress + Settings(non-danger) + Subscription**: ~18.
5. **Wave E — Plan sheets + Food-search + Recap + Digest + Household + Portion-picker + hydration**: ~22.
6. **Wave F — Fasting + Health-sync + Targets + Weight + Profile + What's-new**: ~12.
7. **Wave G — Destructive cluster** (Delete account / Remove household / Log out — after the `destructive` variant decision + implementation).
8. **Wave H — Auth + Onboarding** (judgment lanes; Apple stays HIG; onboarding per its own design — smallest blast radius, last).
